calendar.matters: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Continued To Jul-15-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER FEDERICO MELENDEZ-DOMINGUEZ'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioner's petition to compel arbitration is continued to July 15, 2026. Petitioner and respondent (Mobilitas Insurance Company) agree that this underinsured motorist bodily injury claim is subject to arbitration. (Salazar Decl., par. 12, Ex. C; Zhou Decl., par. 10, Ex. 1; Insurance Code sec. 11580.2(f).) Notwithstanding that agreement and almost six months after petitioner's formal demand, the arbitration process has yet to commence. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding (1) the selection of an arbitrator; (2) exchanging discovery; and (3) coordinating an arbitration schedule. On or before July 1, 2026, each party may file a brief up to seven pages that identifies the parties' progress and any outstanding disputes. The court anticipates that the parties will resolve these issues and take the continued hearing off calendar. If the parties have not agreed to an arbitrator by the continued hearing date, they are ordered to provide three names each of acceptable arbitrators and the court will make a selection.

The motion is moot as to respondent Lyft, Inc. Plaintiff dismissed Lyft, Inc. on April 27, 2026. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
